title: Part 2: The template trap, tool-call correctness vs chat-template formatting

cues.label: Ten percent
cues.headline: Blame fell on the model
cues.narration: The launch tool call pass rate was about ten percent, and the room assumed the weights were weak.

cues.label: The template
cues.headline: Serialize the conversation
cues.narration: The chat template lays out roles, turn delimiters, and special tool tokens into the exact sequence the model was trained on.

cues.label: Wrong tokens
cues.headline: Unparseable tool call
cues.narration: When the template emits the wrong control tokens, the output cannot be parsed as a valid tool call and scores as a failure.

cues.label: Two layers
cues.headline: Capability versus protocol
cues.narration: Capability lives in the weights and protocol lives in the template, so a protocol bug hides a capable model.

cues.label: The fix
cues.headline: Diagnose, do not assume
cues.narration: A hand tuned template lifted the pass rate, and the agentic benchmark caught the nuance single turn evals missed.
